demand_forecast.basis: stated planning assumption for the Scenario E exercise (no production traffic exists); carried as an assumption below

recommended_topology.baseline: 1x g5.xlarge (hardware-a10g-g5-xlarge) replica running vLLM 0.9.1 with the flags below — the measured topology of res-2026-07-10-chatshort-vllm-a10g-001 (per-replica goodput 7.41 rps at 8 rps offered, knee estimate 11.5 rps)
recommended_topology.change_summary: scale out 1 -> 3 replicas on the same hardware profile and engine configuration to serve the 18 rps forecast peak within slo-chat-interactive

uncertainty.method: linear per-replica scaling from the single-replica pooled measurement (3 x 7.41 rps), minus an assumed 3% routing/imbalance penalty; interval from the measured run-to-run spread plus an assumed +/-5% scaling-model error
measured single-replica p95 (0.42) at comparable per-replica load (6 rps/replica at forecast peak vs 8 rps measured), widened by the assumed routing-imbalance margin
measured single-replica p95 carried over (e2e dominated by generation length, not fleet size); interval from measured run-to-run spread
deterministic: 3 x 1.006 USD/h on-demand rate from cost-g5-xlarge-ondemand@0.1.0 (rate itself is source-reported as of 2026-07-10 — re-verify at use time)
3.018 USD/h divided by predicted goodput interval (19.8-22.8 rps) converted to requests/hour

autoscaling.notes: queue wait is the earliest gateway-side saturation signal (admission-enqueue to dispatch, Contract 2 measurement points) and is engine-agnostic; engine-side alternatives (queue_waiting_requests via the capability name_mapping) are viable but couple the autoscaler to engine metric availability. Asymmetric sustain windows damp flapping on the bursty tail.

assumptions: goodput scales linearly with replica count behind least-loaded routing, degraded by a 3% imbalance penalty (assumed, not yet measured at 3 replicas)
traffic matches workload chat-short@0.1.0 (arrival mix, length distributions, no prefix sharing) — the measured basis of the input benchmark result
no cross-replica prefix-cache sharing exists or is modeled (per-replica caches only)
demand forecast peak of 18 rps is a stated planning assumption, not measured traffic
engine configuration, model revision, and hardware are held exactly at the measured pins (any change invalidates the scaling basis per the benchmark comparability rule)
sensitivity_notes: most sensitive to output-length distribution: a +20% shift in mean output tokens reduces per-replica goodput by roughly 15% and moves the recommendation to 4 replicas at the same forecast
knee estimate of the input result carries confidence 0.8 from a 6-point rate sweep; if the true knee is below 10 rps/replica, 3 replicas cannot hold 18 rps within SLO
switching the cost basis to the spot rate (assumed 0.42 USD/h) cuts cost ~58% but adds unmodeled preemption risk (drain semantics of fs-05 limit, not eliminate, the impact)
the 3% routing-imbalance penalty is assumed; if measured imbalance exceeds ~10%, the goodput lower bound no longer covers the forecast peak

re_measurement.single_declared_variable: replica_count 1 -> 3 (engine flags, model revision meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct@0e9e39f2, hardware profile, workload version+seed, and warm-up policy all held at the pins of res-2026-07-10-chatshort-vllm-a10g-001, per the benchmark comparability rule)
success_criteria: pooled goodput >= 19.8 rps (prediction lower bound) at 18 rps offered, evaluated against slo-chat-interactive@0.1.0
shed_rate and stall_rate reported adjacent to goodput remain < 0.02 (goodput not bought by shedding or stalling)

notes: Example fixture; all values illustrative. Scenario E chain: benchmark result res-2026-07-10-chatshort-vllm-a10g-001 (examples/benchmark/result.json) -> this recommendation -> topology change (baseline 1 replica -> 3 replicas; deployment shape per examples/deployment/vllm-backend.json) -> re_measurement plan above.
